topic: Quantum computing applications in cryptography post 2022
final_review:
The advent of quantum computing has significant implications for cryptography, particularly in the post-2022 era (Fernández‐Caramés, 2019; Fernández‐Caramés & Fraga‐Lamas, 2020). Researchers have been exploring the applications of quantum computing in cryptography, with a focus on developing quantum-resistant cryptosystems (Fernández‐Caramés, 2019; Alagic et al., 2022). The selection of cryptographic key sizes is a critical aspect of quantum computing applications in cryptography, as highlighted by Lenstra and Verheul (2001) is not applicable here, however, key size is an important consideration (Fernández‐Caramés, 2019).

In the context of 6G, researchers have been investigating the potential applications of quantum computing in cryptography (Jiang et al., 2021; de Alwis et al., 2021). The development of 6G technologies is expected to have a significant impact on various applications, including vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communications (Noor‐A‐Rahim et al., 2022) and the Internet of Things (IoT) (Tariq et al., 2023). However, the integration of quantum computing with 6G technologies also raises concerns about cybersecurity, particularly in the context of IoT security (Tariq et al., 2023; Kumar et al., 2019).

The use of blockchain technology is another area where quantum computing applications in cryptography are being explored (Fernández‐Caramés & Fraga‐Lamas, 2020; Habib et al., 2022). Blockchain technology has the potential to provide secure and transparent transactions, but it is also vulnerable to quantum computing attacks (Fernández‐Caramés & Fraga‐Lamas, 2020). Researchers have been investigating the development of post-quantum blockchain systems that can resist quantum computing attacks (Fernández‐Caramés & Fraga‐Lamas, 2020).

There are some agreements among researchers regarding the potential applications of quantum computing in cryptography, particularly in the context of 6G and IoT (Jiang et al., 2021; de Alwis et al., 2021; Tariq et al., 2023). However, there are also some contradictions and research gaps, particularly regarding the development of quantum-resistant cryptosystems and the integration of quantum computing with 6G technologies (Alagic et al., 2022; Noor‐A‐Rahim et al., 2022). Further research is needed to address these gaps and to explore the potential applications of quantum computing in cryptography.

References:
Alagic, G., Apon, D., & Cooper, D. A. (2022). Status report on the third round of the NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standardization process. https://doi.org/10.6028/nist.ir.8413

Fernández‐Caramés, T. M. (2019). From Pre-Quantum to Post-Quantum IoT Security: A Survey on Quantum-Resistant Cryptosystems for the Internet of Things. https://doi.org/10.1109/jiot.2019.2958788
Fernández‐Caramés, T. M., & Fraga‐Lamas, P. (2020). Towards Post-Quantum Blockchain: A Review on Blockchain Cryptography Resistant to Quantum Computing Attacks. https://doi.org/10.1109/access.2020.2968985
Habib, G., Sharma, S., & Ibrahim, S. (2022). Blockchain Technology: Benefits, Challenges, Applications, and Integration of Blockchain Technology with Cloud Computing. https://doi.org/10.3390/fi14110341

Tariq, U., Ahmed, I., & Bashir, A. K. (2023). A Critical Cybersecurity Analysis and Future Research Directions for the Internet of Things: A Comprehensive Review. https://doi.org/10.3390/s23084117
